_comment: Fixture for migration 029_exit_triggers_also_true.sql — schema-derived, zero hardcoded producer values.
_provenance: captured-from-source: field names, types, and constraints derived directly from the mission brief and database.py record_exit_trigger call at alpha_bot_execution.py:1783-1797.

round_trip_cases.description: Two co-firing layers stored as JSON array; round-trips via json.loads back to the original list.
Empty co-fire list (single trigger wins cleanly) stored as '[]', not NULL.
None input stores NULL — no co-fire information available (e.g. legacy rows, pre-029 data).

gate_state_blob_design.rationale: Option A (dual-storage): also_true remains in gate_state_json blob AND is written to the dedicated also_true_json column. The two values are populated from the same source and cannot diverge. The dedicated column is the queryable source of truth for exit-attribution analysis; the blob copy is legacy context. The 4 existing GREEN tests (test_trigger_priority_dispatch.py) assert 'also_true' in gate_state and must stay GREEN unchanged.
